component.name: Jellyx
component.summary: Desktop background music player for people who work with music on

description.p: Jellyx is a desktop background music player built with Rust, Tauri, and Svelte. It lets you listen to YouTube, SoundCloud, and local files in a native desktop app without accounts, subscriptions, or unnecessary video playback.

Jellyx was built for long work sessions where the music matters more than the video. It keeps playback focused on audio while still offering queueing, local organization, and FFT-driven visualizers when you want a richer desktop player view.

ul.li: Listen to YouTube and SoundCloud in an audio-first desktop workflow
Play local files alongside remote sources
Queue management, playlists, artist favorites, and listening history
Real-time audio visualizations including spectrum and oscilloscope modes
No account requirement and no Jellyx subscription model
Automatic yt-dlp management on first use
Full platform release — AppImage, .deb, .rpm, DMG, MSI, NSIS
Remote FFT visualization for YouTube and SoundCloud
Initial Linux release packaging for Jellyx
YouTube and SoundCloud search and playback
Playlists, artist favorites, and recent items on the home screen
Automatic yt-dlp download on first remote use
Native audio playback and visualization pipeline

screenshot.caption: Home screen for returning to recent background music sessions
Search YouTube and SoundCloud from one desktop player
Playback controls and queue for long listening sessions
Save playlists and favorites for repeated work sessions
